name: Brisket with Sweet Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Parsnips, peeled; cut into half-inch slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in the cooker.
2. Over high heat, brown the meat well on both sides, lifting it up frequently with tongs or a spatula to prevent sticking. Add additional fat if needed. Set aside.
3. In the remaining oil in the cooker, sauté the celery, carrots, and onion for 3 minutes, stirring occasionally and taking care to scrape up any browned bits of meat or vegetable from the bottom of the pot.
4. Add the beef stock, water, cinnamon, nutmeg, and allspice. Submerge the rack into this mixture and place the meat on it with the fat side facing up. Lock the lid in place and over high heat bring to high pressure.
5. Maintain high pressure and cook for 55 minutes.
6. Let the pressure drop naturally for about 10-15 minutes. Remove the lid, tilting it away from you to allow any excess steam to escape. If the brisket is tender enough, a small chunk of meat should easily pry loose with a fork. If not sufficiently tender, lock the lid back into place and return to high pressure for another 10-15 minutes.
7. Cool the brisket to room temperature and refrigerate it overnight if time permits. Remove any congealed fat and return the gravy to the cooker.
8. About 20 minutes before serving, stir in enough cider to equal approximately 2 cups. Add the sweet potatoes, parsnips, and prunes. Lock the lid in place and over high heat bring to high pressure. Adjust the heat and cook for 3 minutes.
9. Quick release the pressure. Season with salt and pepper.
10. Cut the brisket into slices on the diagonal, place it back in the cooker, and spoon gravy and vegetables on top. Cover but do not lock. Cook over medium heat just until the meat is warmed through.
11. Arrange the meat and vegetables on a platter and pour the gravy over the top.
